input_dict.agent_role: Summarize first-hop evidence and expose the bridge entity, missing fact, or retrieval direction needed for the next hop.
input_dict.base_prompt: Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

delta_p.trigger: If the question names multiple specific films and one or more passages contain an actor entry that lists some (but not all) of those films in its credits.
delta_p.preserve_rule: Keep the current behavior of extracting the actor name and citing film credits verbatim from the provided passage(s). Preserve concise, first-hop evidence style (do not attempt multi-hop verification here).
delta_p.change_rule: In the summary output, explicitly (a) state the actor name found in the passages and list which of the question's films are present in that passage's credited films (quote or paraphrase the credits as shown); (b) mark which film title(s) from the question are NOT present in those credited films and label them as 'missing verification' or the bridge that needs checking; (c) provide a concrete next-hop retrieval direction using a short search/query template, e.g. 'fetch "<missing film>" page' or 'search "<actor> filmography"' or 'search "<missing film> cast"'. Do not assert the missing film is confirmed — only request verification.
delta_p.drop_or_avoid_rule: Do not present a final answer or claim that the actor appears in any film that was not confirmed by the provided passages. Do not hallucinate or add facts beyond the visible passages. Avoid long verbatim copies of unretrieved pages; keep the summary concise and focused on evidence and the missing link.
delta_p.output_contract: Produce a short `summary` containing three parts: (1) first-hop evidence — actor name and which of the question's films are confirmed in the provided passages; (2) bridge / missing fact — explicit identification of which film(s) from the question remain unverified in the passages; (3) next-hop retrieval direction — one- or two-line search/query suggestions (templates) to confirm the missing film(s). The summary must not assert final answers or unrelated details.
delta_p.why_local_transition: The base prompt resulted in returning an actor name but did not expose which film(s) from the question remained unverified or how to retrieve that verification. This small behavioral change makes the agent explicitly surface the missing verification and give a concrete next retrieval direction, enabling a correct second hop without changing general summarization style.

visible_agent_inputs.question: What actor has appeared in the films "Hamlet", "The Woodlanders" and "The Holiday"?
passages: Borthwood | Borthwood is a hamlet on the Isle of Wight, adjacent to Borthwood Copse, the National Trust woodland.  Borthwood includes some holiday cottages and a pet kennel.  At the 2011 Census the Post Office specified that the population of the hamlet was included in the civil parish of Newchurch, Isle of Wight.

Andrzej Fedorowicz (actor) | Andrzej Fedorowicz (born 21 January 1942) is a Polish actor.  He has made over 10 appearances in film.  He starred in the 1978 comedy film "What Will You Do When You Catch Me? ".

Creagen Dow | Creagen Dow (born May 1, 1991) is an American/Canadian actor, writer, producer who had a recurring role as Jeremiah Trottman on the Nickelodeon series "Zoey 101".  He has also guest starred on "The Big Bang Theory", "Rizzoli & Isles", "Hannah Montana", "Castle", "Entourage", "Hot in Cleveland" and "", to name a few.  Creagen received a Best Supporting Actor award for his role in the feature film "Hamlet's Ghost" and also appeared alongside Reese Witherspoon and Vince Vaughn in "Four Christmases", with Sean Astin in "Amazing Love" and provided the voice of "Mullet Boy" in the film "The Ant Bully".  Creagen can also be seen in various television commercials including the GEICO Horror Movie - It's What You Do campaign.

Rufus Sewell | Rufus Frederik Sewell ( ; born 29 October 1967) is an English actor.  In film, he has appeared in Kenneth Branagh's rendition of "Hamlet" (1996) playing Fortinbras, "The Woodlanders", "Dangerous Beauty", "Dark City", "A Knight's Tale", "The Illusionist", "Tristan and Isolde", and "Martha, Meet Frank, Daniel and Laurence".  On television, he starred as "Obergruppenführer" John Smith in "The Man in the High Castle", an Italian detective in the BBC's television series "Zen" (2011) and also appeared in the mini-series "The Pillars of the Earth" (2010).  In 1993 he played the hero, Will Ladislaw, in the BBC's adaptation of George Eliot's "Middlemarch".  In 2003, he appeared in the lead role in "".  He starred in the CBS drama "Eleventh Hour", which was cancelled in April 2009.  On stage, he originated the role of Septimus Hodge in Tom Stoppard's "Arcadia" and the role of Jan in Stoppard's "Rock 'n' Roll"; the latter earned him an Olivier Award and a Tony Award nomination.

Linda Regan | Linda Regan (born 1949) is a British author and actress, who has appeared in television and film.  She is best known for her role as Yellowcoat April in the British holiday camp sitcom "Hi-de-Hi! ".  She is married to the actor Brian Murphy.

Mike Estime | Mike Estime is an American actor and comedian.  He has made appearances in the film "Last Holiday" (2006) and the sitcom "Talk to Me" (2000) as well as the hit television series "Everybody Hates Chris" as Risky, a street entrepreneur, and on the BET comedy show "Comic View".

Frank Campeau | Frank Campeau (December 14, 1864 – November 5, 1943) was an American actor.  He appeared in 93 films between 1911 and 1940 and made many appearances in films starring Douglas Fairbanks.  He was born in Detroit, Michigan and died in Woodland Hills, Los Angeles.

input_dict.output_requirement: Return one summary_1 string. Summarize the visible hop1 passages and expose a useful second-hop retrieval direction.

request.rendered_prompt:

Use the unchanged base instruction p together with the complete ORDERED
list of local prompt deltas. The deltas are supplemental behavior guidance
for this sample; they are not outputs and must not be quoted mechanically.

This is a direct delta-context execution probe:
- Do not rewrite, aggregate, or synthesize a new prompt p'.
- Apply every delta in order to the visible inputs.
- Judge success only by producing the endpoint agent output.
- Do not attempt to reproduce or discuss intermediate states.
- Do not infer hidden information from the existence of the deltas.
- Use only the visible agent inputs as factual evidence.
- Never output gold/oracle labels, evaluation metrics, or hidden support.
- Preserve the agent's normal input/output contract.
